n.description: Sample size — the number of stamped circuits chosen from the day's production. Used to compute the mean (μ = np = 12) and standard deviation (σ = √(np(1−p)) ≈ 3.36) for the normal approximation.

p.description: The acceptable defective rate (6%). Used together with n to compute the mean and standard deviation for the normal approximation to the binomial distribution.

x.description: The threshold number of defective circuits. The answer computes P(x ≥ 20) using the normal approximation with a continuity correction (19.5).
